what: Extract the inline `lotdToken` derivation from `flake.nix` (currently defined around lines 31–80 as `lotdToken = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "lotd-token" ''...'';`) into a new file `nix/lotd-token.nix`. The new file should follow the same convention as `nix/pi.nix`: a top-level function taking the attrs it needs (`{ pkgs }:`, since the script references `pkgs.jq`, `pkgs.coreutils`, `pkgs.openssl`, `pkgs.curl`, and `pkgs.writeShellScriptBin`) and returning the `writeShellScriptBin` derivation. In `flake.nix`, replace the inline definition with `lotdToken = import ./nix/lotd-token.nix { inherit pkgs; };`, preserving the surrounding comment describing what the token generator does (either moved into the new file or kept at the import site). All existing references to `lotdToken` (`lotdCredentialHelper`, the `gh` wrapper, and the commented-out lines) must continue to resolve unchanged.
why: The `lotdToken` GitHub App token-generator derivation is a self-contained ~50-line shell script embedded inline in `flake.nix`, which bloats the flake and mixes packaging wiring with derivation detail. The repo already established the pattern of moving such derivations into standalone `nix/*.nix` files (e.g. `nix/pi.nix` imported via `import ./nix/pi.nix {...}`); moving `lotd-token` there keeps `flake.nix` focused on composition and makes the token generator easier to locate and reason about independently.

tasks.title: Move lotdToken derivation into nix/lotd-token.nix
tasks.goal: Relocate the inline lotdToken shell-script derivation out of flake.nix into a new nix/lotd-token.nix file that follows the nix/pi.nix convention (a function taking { pkgs } and returning the writeShellScriptBin derivation), and import it in flake.nix via `import ./nix/lotd-token.nix { inherit pkgs; }`. Keep the descriptive comment about the token generator. This is purely a structural move with no behavioral change.
tasks.acceptance: nix build succeeds. The lotd-token script content is byte-for-byte equivalent to before (same appId/installId/privateKeyPath handling, JWT construction, and token exchange). flake.nix no longer contains the inline writeShellScriptBin "lotd-token" block and instead imports it from ./nix/lotd-token.nix. All references to lotdToken (lotdCredentialHelper, gh wrapper) still resolve.
tasks.constraints: Do not change the script's behavior, its output, or its interface. Preserve the explanatory comment. Match the parameter-passing style used by nix/pi.nix. Do not alter unrelated parts of flake.nix.
